title: Research in 10 minutes

spec.title: Research in 10 minutes
spec.notes_for_generator: Competitive landscape on a topic. Teach the learner to use LLM research as structure and hypothesis generation, with explicit verification flags and concrete source-checking steps. Do not teach self-confidence as truth.

content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 6: Research a Market in 10 Minutes

Most research tasks don't need a consultant. They need a decent map and the sense to know what's still missing.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
───────────────
🔎 RESEARCH | 🔵 STEADY STATE | ⏱ 12–18 min

THE BRIEF:
You need a quick competitive landscape on a topic — a market, an industry, a product category, or a space relevant to your work. You have ten minutes before a meeting, a planning session, or a conversation where you need to sound oriented rather than lost. Your job is to use the AI to generate a structured map of the space: who's in it, what they're doing, and where the fault lines are. The output is a hypothesis, not a verdict.

YOUR REPS:
1. Pick a market, category, or competitive space you're genuinely curious about (or currently working on). Ask the AI to map the competitive landscape — but give it a frame. For example: "Explain this as if I'm arriving new to the space and need to understand the main players, how they differ, and where competition is actually happening." Include the topic, any geographic or sector constraints, and one angle you specifically care about (pricing, technology, customer type, etc.). Write the prompt yourself — don't just name the topic and stop there.

2. Read the output and mark it up — mentally or physically. Flag anything that feels like it might be outdated, overconfident, or that you'd want to verify before repeating in a meeting. Then ask the AI directly: "Which parts of this are most likely to have changed in the last 12–18 months, and what should I check before relying on them?" Compare its answer to your own flags.

3. Ask the AI to give you three hypotheses about where this space is heading — framed explicitly as hypotheses, not predictions. Then ask it to identify one assumption buried in each hypothesis that could be wrong. Use this to pressure-test whether the map it gave you earlier is a useful starting point or something you'd need to significantly update before acting on it.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
• The landscape map distinguishes meaningfully between players — not just a list of names, but a sense of how they differ and where the real competition sits
• You can point to at least two specific claims in the output you'd want to verify before using them in a professional context
• The hypotheses in Rep 3 are falsifiable — they make a specific enough claim that you could imagine evidence that would prove them wrong
• Your verification flags from Rep 2 and the AI's own flags meaningfully overlap — if they don't, that gap itself tells you something worth noting

💡 COACHING NOTE:
LLMs are good at structure and plausible pattern-matching; they are not good at being current. The most useful thing you can do with AI-generated research is treat it as a first draft of a map — useful for orientation, dangerous as a source. The move here is to use the AI's own awareness of its limitations against itself: asking it to flag what might be stale forces it to reason about its knowledge rather than just deploy it. This transfers everywhere. Any time you're using an AI to produce factual claims — market data, regulatory detail, personnel, pricing — run a verification step as a standard rep, not an afterthought.

──────────
Thinking about how this fits into the rest of your week? Days 1–14 are free. After that, The 90-Day AI Habit is the longer runway — structured daily practice across writing, strategy, research, and management that builds into a working portfolio. You can join solo, bring a small group at a discount, or put it to your employer. Details here: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/

Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive.
Share the programme: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
Found this useful? You can leave a tip: https://ko-fi.com/theaihabit
review_notes: Clarified that AI Habit focuses on chat-based large language models (LLMs), while using AI as public shorthand.
Kept exercises model-agnostic and usable with any text-based LLM or approved work tool.
Reworked research/reasoning language to avoid over-trusting model confidence or hidden chain-of-thought.
Reframed AI Habit as a grounded free 14-day starter block with a paid continuation for deeper 60-90 day habit-building, rather than a validation experiment.

Positioned the paid continuation as The 90-Day AI Habit: a structured applied-LLM programme covering writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs.
Simplified the 90-day commercial route: join individually, bring a small group for a discounted group subscription, or use the workplace launch pack to get an employer to pay.
Added ethical upgrade nudges to Days 3, 7, and 10: endowed progress, career identity, and momentum protection for The 90-Day AI Habit.
Mechanical repair pass: normalised workout badge line after scheduler verification.
Regenerated on 2026-06-05 because original scheduled source was missing core workout sections.
